events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need produce JSON answer. Need analyze scenario: field service, overcurrent device selection & coordination. Observed field condition: breaker/conductor ampacity mismatch. Work staged. Need select correct tool/material/replacement approach and explain why observed choice is wrong. The observed choice is wrong because breaker rating exceeds conductor ampacity, violating NEC 240 overcurrent protection requirements. Need decision fail, risk high/critical? Breaker/conductor mismatch can cause conductor overheating, fire. Risk high or critical? Since installed-defective and non-compliant. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Work stage staged. Percent complete maybe 0. Value maybe 0. Need maybe include value as ampacity mismatch? Not asked numeric reading. Could set 0. Findings: overcurrent device rating exceeds conductor ampacity; conductor not protected per NEC 240.4; potential fire hazard. Actions: replace breaker with correct ampacity matching conductor/termination ratings and load; verify conductor size, termination temp ratings per 110.14(C), AFCI/GFCI per 210.12/210.8 if applicable; coordinate with upstream devices. Rationale: breaker must protect conductor per ampacity; mismatch causes conductor overload without tripping. References NEC 240.4, 110.14(C), 210.8, 210.12. Need ensure JSON valid. Also include workable maybe empty list? Since staged and not begun, maybe activities that can start: inspect, replace breaker, test. But task asks workable for list of activity IDs when asks what work can still start. Not specifically asked. Could include empty list. Order empty. Sound source etc default empty/zero. Need no prose.

events.label: response
events.text: {"decision":{"decision":"fail","risk":"high","work_stage":"staged","component_conditions":["installed-defective","non-compliant"],"percent_complete":0,"value":0,"sound_source":"","confidence":1.0,"event_time":0.0,"rate":0.0,"order":[],"workable":[],"findings":["Circuit breaker ampacity exceeds conductor ampacity, creating an overcurrent protection mismatch.","Conductor is not protected against overload per NEC 240, risking overheating and fire.","Termination temperature ratings may not support the existing breaker/conductor combination per NEC 110.14(C)."],"actions":["De-energize the circuit and verify the conductor size, insulation rating, and termination temperature limits.","Select and install a replacement overcurrent device with ampacity equal to or less than the conductor ampacity and coordinated with upstream/downstream devices.","Confirm AFCI/GFCI requirements per NEC 210.12 and 210.8 are met for the circuit.","Re-energize only after testing and confirming proper operation."],"rationale":"An overcurrent protective device must be sized to protect the conductor based on its ampacity. A breaker larger than the conductor ampacity allows the conductor to overheat before the breaker trips, violating NEC 240 and creating a fire hazard. The replacement breaker must also match termination temperature ratings per NEC 110.14(C) and be coordinated with the rest of the system.","references":["NEC 240","NEC 110.14(C)","NEC 210.8","NEC 210.12"]}}
